results.subject: Weekly Thing for August 5, 2017

findings.exact_snippet: But he beat me—and I was early. *### [Quantify everything with Exist.io custom tracking - BrettTerpstra.com](http://brettterpstra.com/2017/08/02/quantify-everything-with-exist-dot-io-custom-tracking/)

findings.suggested_fix: Remove the stray `*` and ensure each `###` heading starts on its own line so the following link items render correctly.

findings.exact_snippet: "Distributed systems are never 'up'; they exist in a constant state of partially degraded service."* There is a lot of truth there.
findings.why: A stray `*` appears mid-sentence, a leftover from the broken formatting run that merged multiple link entries.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the stray asterisk and restore the proper heading/paragraph structure for the affected link entries.

findings.suggested_fix: Re-host the EFF logo on a stable URL or replace with a valid asset.

results.overall_assessment: The issue has a significant formatting break in the Links section where a stray asterisk collapses ~10 link entries into one run-on paragraph, which should be fixed; a broken promo image also persists.

findings.exact_snippet: Netlify [shares how they moved from Lunr.js](https://lunrjs.com) [search using JavaScript to Algolia](https://twitter.com/anna_picchetti)
findings.why: The link anchor text is split awkwardly across two links with mismatched URLs — 'search using JavaScript to Algolia' points to a Twitter profile instead of Algolia, suggesting the links got mangled during migration.
findings.suggested_fix: Rework the sentence so the link text matches the URLs, or re-point the second link to the correct Algolia/Netlify destination.

findings.exact_snippet: There is a lot [of room to improve! Thanks @anna_picchetti](https://twitter.com/anna_picchetti) for the link!
findings.why: The link bracket starts mid-sentence at '[of room to improve!' which is clearly a mis-placed bracket from migration — the link text should just be '@anna_picchetti'.
findings.suggested_fix: Move the opening bracket so only '@anna_picchetti' is the linked text.

findings.exact_snippet: [they could combine a Circuit Breaker](https://www.martinfowler.com/bliki/CircuitBreaker.html) [, a Bulkhead](https://www.amazon.co.uk/dp/1680502395/)
findings.why: Link anchor text brackets are placed mid-phrase in a way that includes stray words like 'they could combine a' and leading comma/space ', a' inside the link text — a migration artifact.
findings.suggested_fix: Re-anchor the links so only 'Circuit Breaker' and 'Bulkhead' are the linked text.

findings.exact_snippet: I [went ahead and created a security.txt](https://links.thingelstad.com/security.txt) [anyway. It complements my robots.txt](https://links.thingelstad.com/robots.txt) [and humans.txt](https://links.thingelstad.com/humans.txt) .
findings.why: Link anchor text absorbs surrounding prose ('went ahead and created a', 'anyway. It complements my', 'and') — classic migration artifact where link boundaries shifted.
findings.suggested_fix: Re-anchor so only 'security.txt', 'robots.txt', and 'humans.txt' are the linked text.

findings.exact_snippet: [what they include by default. Brave](https://www.brave.com)
findings.why: Link anchor text includes the trailing prose 'what they include by default.' before 'Brave' — the bracket should only enclose 'Brave'.
findings.suggested_fix: Move the opening bracket so only 'Brave' is linked.

findings.exact_snippet: In some ways this reminds [me of a modern day lynx](http://lynx.invisible-island.net) browser
findings.why: Link anchor text spans unrelated words ('me of a modern day lynx') rather than just 'lynx'.
findings.suggested_fix: Re-anchor so only 'lynx' is linked.

findings.exact_snippet: Nobody things of Apple as a semiconductor company.
findings.why: 'things' should be 'thinks'.
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'things' to 'thinks'.

findings.exact_snippet: going in there spam folders
findings.why: 'there' should be 'their'.
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'there' to 'their'.

results.overall_assessment: Readable issue, but contains several migration-era malformed links where anchor text boundaries absorbed surrounding prose, plus a couple of minor typos.

results.subject: Weekly Thing for October 21, 2017

findings.exact_snippet: [I’m a subscriber of Dave Pell](https://twitter.com/davepell) ['s NextDraft](http://nextdraft.com) and really like it. Nice interview talking about how NextDraft came about and highlighting is workflow for making it.
findings.why: The link text is split awkwardly across two separate markdown links creating a broken-looking '[...]'s NextDraft' fragment, a migration artifact from reformatting.
findings.suggested_fix: Merge into a single natural sentence with one link, e.g., 'I’m a subscriber of [Dave Pell](https://twitter.com/davepell)’s [NextDraft](http://nextdraft.com)'.

findings.exact_snippet: One [is a flat array of links](https://links.thingelstad.com/feeds/links.json) [the other is grouped by hostname](https://links.thingelstad.com/feeds/links-by-host.json) .

findings.suggested_fix: Add 'and' between the two links: 'One [is a flat array of links](...) and [the other is grouped by hostname](...)'.

findings.exact_snippet: Make it your strategic more!
findings.why: 'strategic more' appears to be a typo for 'strategic moat'.
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'strategic more' to 'strategic moat'.

findings.exact_snippet: https://archive.org
findings.why: Bare URL on its own line in the Promotion section looks like a leftover image/embed placeholder from migration rather than intentional content.

findings.exact_snippet: https://itunes.apple.com/us/app/copied/id1015767349?mt=8&uo=4&at=1001lxyE&ct=thingelstad_com
findings.why: Bare URL on its own line in the Featured App section appears to be a leftover image embed placeholder from the original Mailchimp layout.

results.overall_assessment: The issue is largely readable but has several migration artifacts around link formatting and bare URLs that once were images/embeds, plus one likely typo ('strategic more').

results.subject: Weekly Thing for October 28, 2017

findings.why: The link text incorrectly spans across a sentence boundary, suggesting the link anchor was misplaced during migration — 'totally DIY' and 'I'll stick with Feedbin' should be separate thoughts.

findings.why: Link anchor spans across a sentence boundary and points to Wikipedia though text says 'backer' — misplaced anchor from migration.
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite so the link text is just 'Wikipedia' on the correct clause.

findings.why: Sentence ends with no terminal punctuation after the link, suggesting a minor truncation.

results.overall_assessment: Readable issue with era-typical style, but several links in the Links and Promotion sections have anchor text that runs across sentence boundaries — likely migration artifacts worth cleaning up.

findings.exact_snippet: ## The history of MarketWatch: How a sports data startup became a half-billion-dollar financial news site - Outside the Box - MarketWatch (https://www.marketwatch.com/(S(rnrsydaynixa5x55oiibxm45))/story/the-history-of-marketwatch-how-a-sports-data-startup-became-a-half-billion-dollar-financial-news-site-2017-10-30)

findings.exact_snippet: When I want to do real [work I jump into Sublime Text](http://www.sublimetext.com) .

results.overall_assessment: Readable but marred by a systemic link-formatting bug where brackets start mid-title throughout Featured/Links/Breadcrumbs sections, plus a few non-canonical section headings.

findings.exact_snippet:
During blustery fall days the autumn leaves cling to the trees, flapping in the wind. You can hear them crinkle as they blur into a rusty orange pile on the ground. 🍁

During blustery fall days the autumn leaves cling to the trees, flapping in the wind.
Oct 20, 2018 at 12:40 PM
findings.why: The caption sentence is duplicated — it appears once as a full paragraph and then again as a stray line before the date/location metadata.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the duplicated caption line so only the date and location appear as metadata after the descriptive paragraph.

findings.exact_snippet: - How Google Protected Andy Rubin, the ‘Father of [Android’ - The New York Times](https://www.nytimes.com/2018/10/25/technology/google-sexual-harassment-andy-rubin.html) www.nytimes.com

findings.suggested_fix: Move the opening bracket to the start of the title: [How Google Protected Andy Rubin, the 'Father of Android' - The New York Times](...).

findings.exact_snippet: - Trek10 [| The Business Case For Serverless](https://www.trek10.com/blog/business-case-for-serverless/)
findings.why: Link anchor text starts with a pipe character because the bracket was placed mid-title, producing awkward rendered text.

findings.exact_snippet: It looks like a we are getting on this train!

findings.suggested_fix: Remove 'a': 'It looks like we are getting on this train!'

findings.exact_snippet: Life After Google: The Fall of Big Data and the Rise of the Blockchain Economy ()
findings.why: Empty parentheses after the book title suggest a template field (author/year) that did not populate.
findings.suggested_fix: Either remove the empty parentheses or fill in the intended metadata.

results.overall_assessment: Readable overall, but the Microposts section contains a badly mangled Mölkky link and the My Weekly Photo section has a duplicated caption line; a couple of 'Yet More Links' entries also have brackets placed mid-title.

findings.exact_snippet: The kids each got [big Lego projects: a Ferris Wheel](https://shop.lego.com/en-US/product/Ferris-Wheel-10247) [and an Old Fishing Store](https://shop.lego.com/en-US/product/Old-Fishing-Store-21310) [. Tammy got an Earth Puzzle](https://n-e-r-v-o-u-s.com/shop/product.php?code=368)
findings.why: Link boundaries are misplaced so punctuation/sentences are swallowed into anchors (e.g., `[. Tammy got an Earth Puzzle]`), producing awkward rendering and sentence breaks.
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrap link text so anchors contain only the thing being linked and the period/sentence break stays outside the link.

findings.exact_snippet: [years. Donate to Let's Encrypt today!](https://letsencrypt.org/donate/)
findings.why: Link text spans a sentence boundary, pulling the word `years.` into the anchor and breaking normal prose flow.

findings.exact_snippet:
## Give Back 🎁

https://letsencrypt.org
findings.why: A bare URL appears immediately under the section header before the linked prose, likely a leftover from editing.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove the redundant bare URL line.

findings.exact_snippet: HIs list of apps
findings.why: Obvious capitalization typo: `HIs` should be `His`.
findings.suggested_fix: Change `HIs` to `His`.

results.overall_assessment: Readable issue with era-normal structure, but the Microposts section contains visible `mp-photo-alt[]=` migration artifacts and the `My Blog Posts ✍️` header lost its `##`, which are the main concerns.

results.subject: Weekly Thing #87 / Jan 5, 2019

findings.exact_snippet: In addition to donating, you should consider making your content under a Creative Commons [license. Donate to Creative Commons today!](https://creativecommons.org/donate/)
findings.why: The markdown link bracket spans across a sentence boundary, making 'license. Donate to Creative Commons today!' all one link when 'license' should likely be outside the link text.
findings.suggested_fix: Restructure so the link text is only 'Donate to Creative Commons today!' and 'license.' ends the prior sentence normally.

findings.exact_snippet: - In 2019, blockchains will start to [become boring - MIT Technology Review](https://www.technologyreview.com/s/612687/in-2019-blockchains-will-start-to-become-boring/)

findings.suggested_fix: Rewrap so the entire article title is the link text, e.g., `[In 2019, blockchains will start to become boring - MIT Technology Review](...)`.

findings.exact_snippet:
- Trillion Dollar [Coach - Eric Schmidt - Hardcover](https://www.harpercollins.com/9780062839268/trillion-dollar-coach/) www.harpercollins.com
Will be looking forward [to reading this book. Via @SamPierson](https://twitter.com/sam_pierson/status/1078476847522988032?s=20) .

findings.suggested_fix: Make the full title the link and limit the Twitter link to 'Via @SamPierson'.

findings.exact_snippet: Didn’t make sense at first a lot of fun after we figured it out.
findings.why: Missing conjunction/punctuation ('but' or a comma) between 'at first' and 'a lot of fun' makes the sentence ungrammatical.
findings.suggested_fix: Insert 'but' or a comma: 'Didn’t make sense at first, but a lot of fun after we figured it out.'

findings.exact_snippet: Turns our New Years Eve is a busy night at theaters!
findings.why: 'Turns our' should be 'Turns out'.
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'Turns our' to 'Turns out'.

findings.exact_snippet: Chores are things you do because your part of the family
findings.why: 'your' should be 'you're' (you are).
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'your' to 'you’re'.

results.overall_assessment: Readable overall, but the 'Yet More Links' and Give Back sections have several malformed markdown link brackets that split titles and commentary, plus a few minor typos in the Microposts.

results.subject: Weekly Thing #88 / Jan 12, 2019

findings.exact_snippet: I've also been playing [around with a (new to me](https://blot.thingelstad.com/2018/12/maybe-blot) [) blogging service called Blot](https://blot.im) .
findings.why: The markdown link syntax is badly broken with parentheses split across two link anchors, producing garbled rendered text like 'around with a (new to me' and ') blogging service called Blot'.
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrite as two cleanly-separated links, e.g., 'playing around with a [(new to me) blogging service called Blot](https://blot.im)' with the 'maybe-blot' reference placed as a separate link.

findings.exact_snippet: Interesting post that reminded me of [Sustainable Energy Without the Hot Air](https://www.withouthotair.com) [, a book my book club](https://rwbook.club) read a while back.
findings.why: The link boundaries are split awkwardly so the anchor text includes leading punctuation (', a book my book club'), which renders as an odd clickable phrase.
findings.suggested_fix: Reflow the links so anchor text is the natural phrase, e.g., '[Sustainable Energy Without the Hot Air](https://www.withouthotair.com), a book [my book club](https://rwbook.club) read...'

findings.exact_snippet: Let's Encrypt is possibly one of the most important things to happen on the web in recent [years. Donate to Let's Encrypt today!](https://letsencrypt.org/donate/)
findings.why: The link anchor awkwardly begins mid-sentence with 'years.' making the rendered clickable text start with a stray word and period.
findings.suggested_fix: Move the link to wrap only the call-to-action phrase: 'in recent years. [Donate to Let's Encrypt today!](https://letsencrypt.org/donate/)'.

findings.exact_snippet: - What I learned at [work this year | Bill Gates](https://www.gatesnotes.com/About-Bill-Gates/Year-in-Review-2018) www.gatesnotes.com
findings.why: The link anchor begins mid-title ('work this year...') instead of wrapping the whole article title, a pattern repeated throughout the 'Yet More Links' section.
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrap each bullet so the full article title is the link anchor, e.g., '[What I learned at work this year | Bill Gates](...)'.

findings.exact_snippet: - Pokémon GO Caught Nearly $800 Million in Global Revenue [Last Year, Growing 35% Over 2017](https://sensortower.com/blog/pokemon-go-revenue-december-2018) sensortower.com
findings.why: Only part of the article title is inside the link, breaking the expected pattern of the full title as anchor text.
findings.suggested_fix: Expand the anchor text to cover the whole headline.

findings.exact_snippet: - How to Delete Online Accounts You [No Longer Need - Consumer Reports](https://www.consumerreports.org/privacy/how-to-delete-online-accounts-you-no-longer-need/) www.consumerreports.org
findings.why: The link anchor starts mid-title, leaving 'How to Delete Online Accounts You' as plain text.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap the full title as the link anchor.

findings.exact_snippet: I will admit this weeks picture is a bit of a stretch though.
findings.why: Missing apostrophe in 'this weeks' (should be 'this week's').
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'this weeks' to 'this week's'.

results.overall_assessment: Content is readable and era-appropriate, but the issue has several malformed markdown links — especially the badly split Blot intro and the 'Yet More Links' bullets where anchor text starts mid-title — that an editor should clean up.

results.subject: Weekly Thing #89 / Jan 19, 2019

findings.exact_snippet: Send an email (mailto:?subject=Check%20out%20the%20Weekly%20Thing&body=Hello!%0A%0AI%20subscribe%20to%20this%20weekly%20email
findings.why: The mailto link uses parentheses instead of markdown link syntax, so 'Send an email' is not a link and the raw mailto URL will render as visible text.

findings.exact_snippet: - More PM Problem [Areas | Silicon Valley Product Group](https://svpg.com/more-pm-problem-areas/) svpg.com
findings.why: The link bracket starts mid-title ('Areas | ...') rather than wrapping the full title, so the rendered link text is truncated — this pattern repeats throughout Yet More Links.
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrap each Yet More Links entry so the entire title is inside the link brackets (e.g., [More PM Problem Areas | Silicon Valley Product Group](...)).

findings.exact_snippet: - mailtolink.me [| markup generator for mailto links](https://mailtolink.me/) mailtolink.me
findings.why: Link bracket begins with '|' leaving 'mailtolink.me' outside the link; rendered link text starts with a stray pipe.
findings.suggested_fix: Rewrap as [mailtolink.me | markup generator for mailto links](https://mailtolink.me/).

findings.exact_snippet: These are handy (mailto:jamie@thingelstad?subject=Mail%20to%20links%20are%20great!&body=What's%20up%3F)

findings.exact_snippet: This is the [best description of what Tim Berners-Lee](https://www.w3.org/People/Berners-Lee/) ['s Solid](https://solid.mit.edu) is about.
findings.why: Split link brackets produce two awkward links with an apostrophe-s dangling between them instead of one coherent link phrase.

findings.exact_snippet: I've been using DuckDuckGo for a long time, and this behind the scenes look at the company [is pretty great. Via Leah Cunningham](http://www.cunningleah.com)
findings.why: The link anchor text 'is pretty great. Via Leah Cunningham' awkwardly combines commentary and attribution into one hyperlink, indicating the intended anchor was just the name.

findings.exact_snippet: This is usable advise that I’m positive I can put to use.
findings.why: 'advise' (verb) should be 'advice' (noun).

results.overall_assessment: Readable overall, but the Yet More Links section and a couple of mailto links have systematically malformed markdown that degrades link rendering.

findings.exact_snippet: My Blog Posts ✍️
findings.why: This should be an H2 section header (## My Blog Posts ✍️) matching the era-standard emoji-H2 pattern, but it's rendered as plain text, making the following H3 an orphan subheading.
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'My Blog Posts ✍️' to '## My Blog Posts ✍️' so the H3 link sits under a proper section.

findings.exact_snippet: The web is a major part of our culture and it the content that we put on it is sadly ephemeral.

findings.suggested_fix: Remove the stray 'it' so the sentence reads 'and the content that we put on it is sadly ephemeral.'

findings.why: The 'Yet More Links' bullets have the link text split mid-title ('The Tech' outside the bracket, 'Revolt — …' inside), a migration artifact that makes link labels read oddly.

findings.exact_snippet: The Internet Archive is working hard to capture that information and keep it for [history. Donate to Internet Archive today!](https://archive.org/donate/)
